titleStmt.title: Saudi Arabia 2022 – Speaker 20 – Shepherd
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Saudi Arabia Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: The baḥám is the young of the sheep, baḥam
I used to graze them, when I was eight years old
eight years old, I was young.
After that, I grew up until I reached the age of eleven
I became a shepherd of sheep, sheep
I was a bit older, I grazed them , in spring, in the desert, I would come
Afterwards, at the age of fourteen, fifteen, I became a camel herder
A camel herder, I would roam in the night, I would bring this there, and this she-camel here and I would come and sleep here
Next to, next to the nāga, the nāga is a type of camel
The nāga, we call the she-camel nāga
A type of camel, we sleep, I sleep here
The offspring(?) Even when they see the fox, they protect me from it
She screeches, she screeches, she starts shaking, do you know?
I stayed with the camels for about fifteen years old.
Afterwards, I stayed almost three years.
I left camel herding. For camel herding we would take two, three months.
We would live on one date and on the camel's milk
The camel could leave maybe for a month and it wouldn't come back to you
I am not worried about it like I am for the sheep
The camel, the fox doesn't approach it, it goes to the younger camels.
The small camel, the child of the she-camel
Or the nāga, we also call it the nāga, the camel
And so, we are not afraid for their safety like we do for the sheep
The sheep, naturally you worry about them, you worry that the fox would approach and eat them, the camel, no, it doesn't come to it
The she-camel separates from her child, she separates from it
and she stays between it and the fox and she shakes and strikes and hits the fox.
so, I lived a life as a shepherd since I was a child
in richness, with the lambs and the sheep and the camels, this is life in the desert
it could be that for the last two months no one would visit me as I was in the desert
and I was young, from the age of fifteen
I would roam with the camels, I had a date, the camels's milk, a bit of wheat and I would put that over the fire
We brought, with me I would have the small bowl and we put the flour like this
I light the fire until it is cooking, I bring the camels milk and I put this wheat in the boiling water, and we eat it
and my health was good, I wanted for nothing
My health was good, until I reached sixteen
after I turned sixteen I started to get educated
I was learning at the late hours, meaning I would go to learn in the evening, in the evening, I only studied in the evening
It was considered as a reward ((not sure about the translation))
No, in the morning no, because we were older, the morning was for the younger children, the eight year olds
they were six year olds and over, we were older, sixteen years old
meaning, we studied by night, for my education, and I studied
I studied writing and reading,and becase I was educated I got my ID
which is the 'belonging ID', the 'belonging ID' ((dont know how to translate this better))
which is the ID, nowadays, we call it the ID
but before we used to call it the 'belonging ID'
the ID
I received my ID and I went and worked
and my education continued up to the secondary level
Afterwards, thank God, I got educated, I studied about life, we got married, we built a family, we built a house
and thank God, life was good
and this is the Bedouin life
It didn't keep us from learning, it didn't keep us from anything that we wanted, we didn't know anything before and we didn't know how to read or write.
however, it never concerned me; we never faced any obstacles in obtaining an education
We learned, we studied life, we experienced life and all of it was a wonder from our beginning here, it is beautiful, beautiful
We, the Bedouins they now love..., I love the camels a lot
I love the camels a lot
all those to belong to us, they all love the camels, thank you.
